target.title: fix: extract large inbound PDFs via canonical media limits, stack-safe attachment parsing
target.body:
## Summary

Canonical fix for #90098, superseding closed PR #90111 per the maintainer review there: instead of adding a parallel inbound-PDF extraction subsystem, this routes the existing media-understanding pipeline's input-file limits through the existing config keys, and keeps the orthogonal stack-safety hardening as small index-based parsers.

- **Inbound extraction limits** (`src/media-understanding/apply.ts`): `resolveFileLimits` now resolves the byte cap as explicit `gateway.http.endpoints.responses.files.maxBytes`, else `max(inbound attachment cap, agents.defaults.pdfMaxBytesMb)` — where the inbound attachment cap is `agents.defaults.mediaMaxMb` (default 20 MB), the same cap the gateway already uses to *accept* attachments. The PDF page cap resolves as explicit `files.pdf.maxPages`, else `agents.defaults.pdfMaxPages`, else 20 (the documented default for that key). `pdfMaxBytesMb` can only raise the cap: anything already accepted at the attachment cap must stay extractable, or the silent marker-only degradation returns for mid-size PDFs. Extraction output stays bounded (`maxChars` 60k) and untrusted-wrapped via the existing `extractFileBlocks` → `extractFileContentFromSource` → `wrapUntrustedAttachmentContent` path. The OpenResponses HTTP endpoint (`src/gateway/openresponses-http.ts`) keeps its own 5 MB / 4-page defaults.
- **Shared cap ownership** (`src/media/configured-max-bytes.ts`): `resolveChatAttachmentMaxBytes` / `DEFAULT_CHAT_ATTACHMENT_MAX_MB` moved out of `src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts` so the gateway accept path and inbound extraction read one canonical cap (media-understanding must not import gateway code). Callers updated; behavior identical.
- **Gateway stack-safe parsing** (`src/gateway/chat-attachments.ts`): `isValidBase64` is now an iterative scan and the data-URL prefix strip is index-based, so multi-megabyte payloads never go through a full-string regex (`isValidBase64` `RegExp.test` is the exact frame in #80679's stack trace). Behavior verified equivalent to the old regexes, including padding-placement edge cases.
- **Control UI stack-safe parsing** (`ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts`, `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`): `dataUrlToBase64` (send path) and `dataImageClipboardFile` (paste path) parse by index instead of full-string regex capture.
- **Stale queue cleanup** (`ui/src/ui/chat/composer-persistence.ts`): failed queued sends whose `sendError` matches `Maximum call stack size exceeded` predate the parser fix and are dropped on persist and restore, so stale failures stop replaying after upgrade.
- Docs/help text for `agents.defaults.pdfMaxBytesMb` / `pdfMaxPages` updated to name the inbound-extraction role.

### Deliberately not included

- **`chat.send` oversized managed-PDF pass-through** (from #90111): omitted. The sandbox staging-cap rejection (`MEDIA_MAX_BYTES`, clear 4xx) is a separate safety boundary; for locked-down-but-unsandboxed agents — the scenario in the original proof — the canonical limit raise alone delivers the extracted text. Per the review, it is largely moot once extraction limits are raised; it can be re-proposed on its own merits for sandboxed sessions if needed.
- **Sibling full-string data-URL regexes** in `src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.ts` (outbound media payloads) and `src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.ts` share the pattern shape but sit on different surfaces (outbound/managed-image); converting them is a mechanical follow-up I'm happy to do in a separate PR rather than widen this one.

### Behavior notes for release

- Inbound PDFs/documents between 5 MB and the attachment cap (default 20 MB) are now extracted (bounded, untrusted-wrapped) instead of reaching the agent as a bare `[media attached: …]` marker; inbound PDF extraction covers up to 20 pages by default instead of 4.
- Data URLs with parameterized MIME (`data:text/plain;charset=utf-8;base64,…`) were previously rejected (gateway) or silently dropped (UI); they now parse — strictly more lenient, MIME params are still stripped by existing normalization.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-understanding/apply.file-limits.test.ts src/gateway/chat-attachments.test.ts` — 7 + 132 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts` — 55 passed (includes new >5 MB PDF extraction, over-cap skip, and `pdfMaxPages` routing behavior tests against the `document-extractors.runtime` boundary)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/composer-persistence.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` — 236 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.sanitize-mime.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts` — all passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:test:src`, `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` — clean
- `pnpm check:import-cycles` — 0 cycles; `oxfmt --check` + oxlint on changed files — clean
- Independent adversarial review of the diff, including brute-force equivalence of the new `isValidBase64` against the old regex over ~11k edge-case strings — no behavioral mismatches.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Large inbound PDFs (>5 MB) were skipped by media-understanding extraction so agents saw only an attachment marker; large Control UI attachment sends failed with `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded` in gateway/UI data-URL parsing; stale failed queue items kept replaying.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout (Linux, Node 24) with the focused suites above. The live-gateway failure mode itself (7.97 MB and 15.6 MB PDF uploads) was demonstrated in #90111's proof on an installed 2026.6.1 gateway; this PR lands the canonical mechanism for the same scenario.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.file-limits.test.ts src/gateway/chat-attachments.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/composer-persistence.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` plus `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:test:src`, `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`, `pnpm check:import-cycles`.
- Evidence after fix: All listed suites green (7/132/55/236 plus consumer suites); new tests pin: a 6 MB PDF extracts through the canonical pipeline with a 20-page cap under default config, an over-cap PDF skips without invoking the extractor, `agents.defaults.pdfMaxPages` routes to extraction, 64 KB+ data URLs parse by index, and stale stack-overflow queue items are dropped on persist and on restore of pre-fix storage.
- Observed result after fix: Inbound PDFs above the legacy 5 MB cap produce bounded, untrusted-wrapped text in the prompt body via `applyMediaUnderstanding`; oversized files still skip; parsing no longer routes multi-megabyte payloads through full-string regexes.
- What was not tested: A fresh end-to-end large-PDF upload against a live production gateway in this session (the same user scenario was live-verified in #90111; the canonical path here is covered by behavior tests at the extractor boundary).

Closes #90098

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
